commands.summary: Backend TypeScript build passed with the provider-neutral voice contract types and adapter hooks.

Channel contract tests passed: 5/5. Coverage includes provider selection, unsupported-capability rejection, SMS send contract, and the voice edge contract for availability, outbound start, inbound call normalization, status normalization, and call end.

Full Electron suite green: 73 tests passed and 1 opt-in live Twilio test skipped by design. Backend build, renderer build, renderer interaction tests, backend node tests, and runtime syntax checks passed.
Local system audit passed: required files, 18 work items, lifecycle counts, stale README checks, work item manifests, and Markdown link/date checks are current.

commands.command: python scripts/repopact_cli.py dashboard && python scripts/repopact_cli.py doctor && python scripts/validate_repo.py
commands.summary: RepoPact records are current: dashboard regenerated, doctor reports healthy, and repository governance validation passed.

commands.command: npm run scan:secrets && npm run scan:deps
commands.summary: Secret scan clean and production dependency audit reports 0 vulnerabilities.

environment.process_note: 015 CLV-012 (voice runtime contracts). ForgeLink now has provider-neutral voice shapes for local call identity, outbound call requests, start/end results, inbound call events, status callbacks, availability/disabled states, call persistence inputs, and redacted provider errors. Voice remains a telecom-edge capability; PSTN calling requires a provider, SIP trunk, carrier partnership, or direct interconnect. Twilio Voice implementation remains CLV-013, and call UI/history remain later criteria.
